title: what I think about multinational coorporations

transcript.text: Hello. This video is what I think about multinational corporations.
A lot of people don't like them. When I was younger, I didn't like them.
DeForest, New Guinea, and all this stuff.

I wanted to listen to the interview again, but I can't find it.
It came about kind of with the growth of the English Empire.
It's kind of more advanced, I think, than just the traditional,
The Queen owns everything and he tells everybody else what to do.
So, now it's kind of more of a democracy.
The Queen, I guess, got all these rich people to kind of pool their money together to do business.
So, that's what corporations, you know, public corporations, right?
You get a bunch of people to invest in something,
To have their money together to do something cool, right?
For the sake of making money, so that they can buy more cars and more houses and everything.
But now, kind of the zeitgeist of the world is that, you know,
They're eating up the world, eating up the world's resources and stuff.
So, let's become a little bit more responsible.
And I'm just thinking about making money so you can buy more stuff.
So, this is kind of, I think, where multinational corporations are starting to go.
Shareholders and starting to be a little bit more responsible.
Because before, I think, the leader of a multinational corporation would feel a responsibility to the shareholder, you know,
So they would have to keep doing stuff just to make money.
Even if it's, you know, stuff like buying out other companies and reducing your prices so that other companies,

Unfair kind of stuff.
So, basically, what should we do to fix multinational corporations?
I know people aren't going to like this.
Somebody might pick this off and they're for saying this.
But I say, you go transparency.
I say, every multinational corporation, you click on their website, and you see their list of shareholders and how much they invested.
So, if you decide you don't like a multinational corporation because they did a bad thing, you get to write those shareholders.
Anybody gets to write those shareholders a letter.
Or send them a video and give them a piece of their mind.
You know, and maybe there's like millions of shareholders, a multinational corporation,
And you don't even know that you're a shareholder of this world's not corporation

And then, you know, somebody writes you, or you send an email and goes,
Hey, you know, you think you're that hippie?
What you doing in being invested in, uh, in this, uh, in Bayer or something?
Not like Bayer's bad, but, you know, I know a lot of these, uh, drug companies, pharmaceutical companies, um,

That's what I say we do, we go, we go travel.

Maybe people become more, um, comfortable on their own.

I, I'm not very well read in this subject.
